title: SAP Systems Architect
description:
Job Scope:

  • Monitor the overall health of SAP systems and perform pre‑emptive actions to prevent disruption to business operations.
  • Monitor and manage batch jobs, user status, spool queues, system logs, locks/deadlocks, and overall system performance.
  • Work closely with the Regional SAP team to support change releases from Development → Quality (Staging) → Production environments.Support and maintain the SAP landscape, including ECC, CRM, BW, EP, APO‑DP, GRC, Virsa, Solution Manager, and any new SAP additions.
  • Perform SAP backup and recovery, SAP security role creation and user assignments, and SAP archiving.
  • Fine‑tune Oracle databases, SAP environments, and AIX operating systems to ensure optimal performance and stability.
  • Provide technical solutioning and architecture support for SAP systems and enhancements.

Requirements:

Technical Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years’ experience in SAP technical / Basis roles.
  • At least one full-cycle SAP ECC6 upgrade experience is mandatory.
  • Hands‑on experience with SAP ECC6 upgrade and SAP XI/PI administration.
  • Proven experience in SAP Solution Manager implementation and configuration.
  • Experience acting as a Solution Architect for technical SAP solutions.
  • Strong working knowledge of SAP NetWeaver concepts and products.
  • Experience with Oracle database performance tuning across SAP systems.
  • Knowledge of high availability (HA) design, including SAP application and database clustering methods.
  • Has set up and implemented at least one end‑to‑end full life‑cycle SAP system.

SAP Functional & Platform Knowledge

  • Knowledge of SAP CRM (preferably CRM 7), SAP BW, APO/DP, Enterprise Portal (EP 7), GRC, and Virsa.
  • Familiarity with AIX operating systems in SAP environments.

Methodology & Delivery

  • Familiar with ASAP Methodology, tools, and delivery techniques.
  • Able to manage scope of work and meet tight deadlines in a production environment.

Skills & Competencies

Customer‑focused with strong problem‑solving skills.
Good written and verbal communication skills.
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively as part of a team.

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

postedCompany.description:
PERSOL is Asia-Pacific’s leading Staffing and HR solutions partner, operating across 13 markets with deep local insight and regional scale. With more than 80 offices and decades of experience, we deliver integrated workforce solutions that are tailored, tech-enabled, and designed for the dynamic world of work.

We combine human expertise with smart technology to help organisations solve workforce challenges, unlock potential, and stay ahead of change. From recruitment and talent management to workforce strategy and advisory, our collaborative approach puts your goals at the centre.

In 2025, we came together under the PERSOL name — reflecting our bold vision for the future of work and our Group’s Vision: Work and Smile.

Whether you’re building teams, growing careers, or transforming how work gets done, we’re here.
